response:
THE LAW OF MOORE, FORMULATED BY GORDON ECKERT MOORE IN 1965, PREDICTS THAT THE NUMBER OF TRANSISTORS ON A MICROPROCESSOR WILL DOUBLE EVERY TWO YEARS. THIS PREDICTION HAS BEEN A DRIVING FORCE IN THE DEVELOPMENT OF COMPUTING TECHNOLOGY FOR DECADES. HOWEVER, AS WE APPROACH THE PHYSICAL LIMITS OF SILICON BASED TECHNOLOGY, IT IS BECOMING CLEAR THAT THE LAW MAY NO LONGER HOLD IN ITS ORIGINAL FORM. SCIENTISTS AND ENGINEERS ARE EXPLORING ALTERNATIVE MATERIALS AND NEW ARCHITECTURES TO CONTINUE THE ADVANCEMENT OF COMPUTING POWER.

*** 

THE FUTURE OF THE LAW OF MOORE DEPENDS ON INNOVATIONS IN NANOTECHNOLOGY, QUANTUM COMPUTING, AND NEW TYPES OF SEMICONDUCTORS. WHILE IT IS DIFFICULT TO PREDICT EXACTLY WHEN THE LAW WILL CEASE TO BE VALID, MANY EXPERTS BELIEVE THAT WE MAY SEE A SHIFT IN THE RATE OF INNOVATION IN THE NEXT DECADE. THE END OF THE LAW OF MOORE DOES NOT MEAN THE END OF TECHNOLOGICAL PROGRESS, BUT RATHER A TRANSITION TO NEW FORMS OF COMPUTING. DO
